name: excel-analysis
description: Analyze Excel spreadsheets, create pivot tables, generate charts, and perform data analysis. Use when analyzing Excel files, spreadsheets, tabular data, or .xlsx files.
capabilities: Read Excel files with pandas read_excel
Process multiple sheets in a workbook
Generate charts and visualizations with matplotlib
Merge and join multiple Excel files
author: davila7
securitySummary: This is a pure documentation skill containing markdown with Python/pandas code examples for Excel analysis. No executable code, scripts, network calls, or filesystem access capabilities are implemented. The static findings are false positives from a malfunctioning scanner that incorrectly flagged markdown code fences as shell commands and normal text as cryptographic algorithms.
valueStatement: Manual Excel analysis takes hours of repetitive work. This skill provides ready-to-use pandas patterns for reading, analyzing, and visualizing spreadsheet data in seconds.
limitations: Does not modify existing Excel files in place
Cannot parse password-protected or encrypted files

useCases.title: Automated Reporting
useCases.description: Automate weekly reports by reading Excel data, generating summaries, and creating charts automatically.

useCases.title: Spreadsheet Cleanup
useCases.description: Clean messy spreadsheets by removing duplicates, handling missing values, and standardizing data formats.

useCases.title: Excel Integration
useCases.description: Integrate Excel data into Python pipelines by learning proper pandas patterns for data extraction.

promptTemplates.title: Basic Reading
promptTemplates.prompt: Read the Excel file data.xlsx using pandas and show me the first rows and basic statistics.

promptTemplates.title: Multi-Sheet
promptTemplates.prompt: Process all sheets from workbook.xlsx and display summary statistics for each sheet.

promptTemplates.title: Pivot Analysis
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a pivot table from sales_data.xlsx grouping by region and product with sum aggregation.

promptTemplates.title: Visualization
promptTemplates.prompt: Read the data, create a bar chart showing sales by category, and save it as chart.png.

bestPractices: Specify sheet_name when reading multi-sheet workbooks to avoid unexpected behavior
Use usecols parameter to read only needed columns for better performance
Convert date columns with pd.to_datetime for proper time-series analysis
antiPatterns: Loading entire large Excel files when only a subset of columns is needed
Not specifying data types, causing pandas to infer types slowly
Modifying the original Excel file instead of saving to a new output file

faq.question: What Python packages are available?
faq.answer: Pandas, openpyxl, xlrd, xlsxwriter, and matplotlib are installed and ready to use.

faq.question: Can this skill read .xls files?
faq.answer: Yes, xlrd is available for reading older .xls format files in addition to modern .xlsx.

faq.question: How do I handle large Excel files?
faq.answer: Use the chunksize parameter or read only specific columns with usecols for better performance.

faq.question: Is my data safe?
faq.answer: This is a documentation skill that shows patterns. Your data is only accessed when you run the code with your files.

faq.question: Can I write formatted Excel files?
faq.answer: Yes, use pandas with openpyxl or xlsxwriter engines for advanced formatting and styling.

faq.question: How does this differ from manual Excel?
faq.answer: Automates repetitive tasks, handles larger datasets, creates reproducible analysis pipelines, and integrates with other Python tools.
